objective: Measure whether genuinely new left-side component-value source families can clear the rpf50 capacity gate for mixed-hook exact support.
non_negotiables: This is a capacity-only diagnostic and must not be treated as selected exact support.
The report must compare every source against the 150-row rpf50 target.
Any capacity-clearing source must still require selector, materialization, replay, leakage, and baseline gates before promotion.
Docs must distinguish new component-value supply from pattern-limit tuning.

tasks.prompt: Add and run a capacity-only left-supply atlas that pairs candidate left-side pattern families with the expanded mixed-hook right side.
acceptance: The report is capacity-only for every source.
The bounded expanded baseline remains at capacity 125.
At least one genuinely new left-supply combination clears the 150-row rpf50 capacity target.
The report records left and right unique component-value counts for every source.

tasks.prompt: Record Wave 62 as a left-supply capacity breakthrough while keeping selected-support and model claims closed.
commands: python3 -m py_compile engine/ml_model.py engine/test.py agents/network.py agents/label_schema.py
acceptance: Docs state that unbounded expanded-plus-outer left supply reaches capacity 150 with 155 left unique values.
Docs state that the unbounded all-left supply reaches capacity 150 with 167 left unique values.
Docs state that the report does not evaluate current selection.
Docs route next work to selector/materialization/replay/leakage gates for the capacity-clearing source.

integration_gates.command: python3 agents/network.py validate && python3 agents/network.py validate-wave agents/waves/wave_62_left_supply_source_atlas.json
integration_gates.success_signal: The Wave 62 plan is internally consistent.
The Wave 62 left-supply atlas is valid JSON.
